title: AI-music firm Udio is recruiting a head of artist partnerships - Music Ally
text:
Fresh from signing licensing deals with Universal Music Group and Warner Music Group, AI-music startup Udio is now looking to build bridges with the music industry in another way:
by recruiting a head of artist partnerships
.
Whoever gets the US-based role will “lead Udio’s relationships with artists, artist managers, labels, and key stakeholders across the music industry… establishing trusted, long-term partnerships with creators and their representatives.”
Duties include encouraging artists to use Udio “from initial conversations through onboarding and ongoing engagement” while also collaborating internally to “structure artist participation frameworks, terms, and workflows” and gather feedback from artists and managers.
Udio is not the only AI-music company going down this path of industry relationships. In July last year the biggest player in the market, Suno,
appointed former Atlantic Records executive Paul Sinclair as its chief music officer
.
Stuart Dredge
Music Ally's Head of Insight
More by Stuart Dredge
